source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Rectangle $W X Y Z$ is cut into four smaller rectangles as shown. The lengths of the perimeters of three of the smaller rectangles are 11, 16 and 19 . The length of the perimeter of the fourth smaller rectangle lies between 11 and 19. What is the length of the perimeter of $W X Y Z$ ? <image1>

asset registry passed and the image file exists, so the diagram dependency is supported by available assets
multi_step: solving likely requires combining perimeter information from multiple sub-rectangles rather than reading a single value
the condition that the fourth small-rectangle perimeter lies between 11 and 19 adds an extra constraint beyond the three given perimeters
the task is geometric/numeric and likely needs at least a few reasoning steps from diagram structure to the whole-rectangle perimeter
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single number ('30'), which is easy to compare against a computed result
the geometry setup is structured enough that a derived perimeter can in principle be checked algebraically from the diagram
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support downstream verification feasibility

although the problem is highly image-dependent, the required diagram asset exists and the registry passed
